#Samsung #OneUI8 #Fonts #GoogleSans #Font

external.description: Great news for Samsung Galaxy users — custom fonts are back! After the March 2026 security patch broke the popular zFont app, the developers have rolled out an update that restores full functionality, and it's simpler to use than ever before. The one catch: you'll need to install a companion app called zFile, made by the same developer. Once your font is installed, it'll appear as a separate package under Settings > Apps, which you can remove at any time.
external.title: Custom Fonts can now be Installed on Samsung One UI 8.0 & 8.5

images.alt:
A surreal portrait of a human figure fills the frame from the shoulders up, set against a soft, desaturated blue background. The figure’s head is tilted slightly back, mouth stretched wide open in a silent scream, revealing teeth and a dark, cavernous interior. The lower half of the face is rendered with smooth, almost sculptural realism, with subtle shading along the neck and jawline that gives the impression of a painted statue brought to life.

Above the nose, the identity fractures. The eyes and upper face are completely obscured by thick, aggressive brushstrokes of paint applied horizontally across the canvas. These strokes blend white, pale blue, muted yellow, and streaks of deep red, creating a chaotic band that replaces the figure’s gaze entirely. The paint appears textured and physical, with visible ridges and smears, as if applied with a palette knife.

The composition creates a stark contrast between control and collapse. The lower face is defined and expressive, locked in a moment of raw emotion, while the upper half dissolves into abstraction, removing individuality and preventing connection. The absence of eyes denies recognition, turning the scream into something internal and unreachable, as if the figure is being erased in real time.

Keywords:
abstract portrait, screaming figure, obscured identity, painterly texture, surreal face, emotional expression, distorted perception, color smear, faceless subject, modern abstraction

A shallow, woven bird’s nest sits low against a muted, mossy-green background that fades into darkness toward the top of the frame. The nest is large and slightly misshapen, built from rough twigs and dried grasses that curl outward at uneven angles. Inside, a cluster of eggs fills the center. Their shells vary in tone, some pale cream, others bluish-green, several cracked open with jagged edges revealing hollow interiors. Tiny fragments of shell are scattered among them, suggesting something recently hatched or broken through.

Intermixed with the eggs are wilted flowers, their petals faded to soft reds and dusty pinks, lying limp across the nest’s surface. Feathers, dark and slightly iridescent, are caught along the rim and woven into the structure, some loose and trailing downward. A faint dusting of golden specks floats in the air and rests lightly on the eggs and twigs, giving the scene a subtle, almost magical shimmer.

The lighting is dim and diffuse, as if filtered through thick foliage or mist, with soft highlights catching the curves of the eggs and the brittle edges of broken shells. The overall composition feels still and heavy, balancing themes of birth and abandonment, with no visible movement or life present.

Keywords:
bird nest, cracked eggs, wilted flowers, feathers, dark green palette, decay, still life, eerie atmosphere, fragile life, nature symbolism

images.alt:
A towering, monstrous figure fills the frame in a close, portrait-style composition. Its face resembles a skull fused with living flesh, pale and bone-like, with deep cracks and pitted textures that suggest age, decay, and battle damage. Two heavy tusks curve upward from its lower jaw, chipped and uneven, framing a grim, clenched mouth. The eye sockets are shadowed, almost hollow, with faint glints suggesting something still alive within.

A dark, tangled mane of coarse hair spills backward, partially obscured by a ragged banner or tattered cloth whipping behind its head, adding motion and violence to the otherwise still pose. The creature’s skin and armor blend together in a murky green-black mass, smeared with grime, blood, and ash. Its shoulders are massive and hunched, giving the impression of immense weight and strength pressing forward.

The background dissolves into a hazy, desaturated wash of pale green and beige, with no clear horizon, making the figure feel isolated and emerging from fog or memory. Fine specks of dust or embers drift across the lower portion, adding texture and atmosphere. The lighting is soft but directional, highlighting the bone structure of the face while leaving the body in shadow, creating a haunting contrast between clarity and obscurity.

Keywords:
orc, skull face, tusks, dark fantasy, war creature, bone texture, grim warrior, monstrous portrait, painterly horror, battlefield relic

Raisins are dried grapes that are a popular snack and ingredient in many dishes around the world. They are made by removing the water from grapes, either by drying them in the sun or by using a dehydrator or oven.
Raisins can be made from a variety of grapes, but the most common types are Thompson seedless, Muscat, and Flame seedless grapes. They are typically small, chewy, and sweet, and have a slightly wrinkled texture.
Raisins are a good source of fiber,
antioxidants, and vitamins and minerals, such as potassium and iron. They are also low in fat and calories, making them a healthy snack option.

National Oatmeal Cookie Day

images.alt:
Oatmeal cookies are a type of cookie that is made with rolled oats, flour, sugar, eggs, and butter or oil. They are a classic American cookie and are popular for their chewy texture and wholesome ingredients.
Oatmeal cookies can be made in a variety of flavors and can include additional ingredients, such as chocolate chips, raisins, nuts, or spices. Some recipes also call for the use of brown sugar or molasses to give the cookies a richer, more complex flavor.
One of the benefits of oatmeal cookies is that they are often considered a healthier alternative to other types of cookies, as oats are high in fiber and contain a number of beneficial nutrients. Additionally, the texture and flavor of oatmeal cookies can be easily
customized to suit individual tastes and preferences.
Oatmeal cookies can be enjoyed as a snack or dessert and are often served with a glass of milk or a cup of cotfee. They can also be used as a base for other desserts, such as ice cream sandwiches or crumbled on top of yogurt or ice cream.
